name: mentat-state-machine-rubric
description: Three-criterion rubric for grading the Mentat state machine, predictive router, and persistence layer. Total weight sums to 1.0; aggregate score = sum(weight_i * score_i / 5.0).

criteria.name: State-Transition Correctness
criteria.description: Given a synthetic event sequence, does the FSA land in the expected end state and traverse the expected intermediate states? The state machine is deterministic — any deviation indicates either a transition-table regression or an event-classification bug.

scoring_guide.0: No scenarios pass. FSA is non-functional or import-broken.
scoring_guide.1: One scenario passes. Transition table is severely degraded.
scoring_guide.2: Roughly half the scenarios pass. Multiple regressions.
scoring_guide.3: Most scenarios pass but at least two fail. One systematic regression.
scoring_guide.4: All but one scenario pass. Localized regression or edge case.
scoring_guide.5: All scenarios pass. FSA is on-spec.

criteria.name: Predictive-Routing Accuracy
criteria.description: After warming the Q-table on a synthetic 200-event trajectory with a known reward distribution, does thompson_recommend pick the empirically-best tool for each state at greater than 70% of the variance-weighted optimum? Cold-start fallback must be graceful (None or first-visit-equivalent). Contested states (two near-equal Q-values with different visit counts) must still allocate meaningful exploration weight (>= 15%) to the higher-variance arm.

scoring_guide.0: No scenarios pass. Recommender is non-functional or always returns None.
scoring_guide.1: One scenario passes. Recommender returns sensible output but selection rate is below random.
scoring_guide.2: Roughly half the scenarios pass. Cold start works but warm-table picks the wrong arm.
scoring_guide.3: Most scenarios pass but exploration is starved (always exploits) or one scenario fails by a wide margin.
scoring_guide.4: All but one scenario pass. Stochastic noise around the 70% threshold.
scoring_guide.5: All scenarios pass. Thompson sampling balances exploration and exploitation as designed.

criteria.name: Persistence-Recovery Integrity
criteria.description: Write FSA state and the Q-table to disk, simulate a crash, reload, and verify the recovered state is bit-identical to what was saved. Also verifies the post-compact handoff IO round-trip in isolation (file produced, parsed back to the same content).

scoring_guide.0: No scenarios pass. Persistence layer is broken — saves but cannot reload.
scoring_guide.1: One scenario passes. Round-trips lose data or corrupt the format.
scoring_guide.2: Roughly half the scenarios pass. One of the three persistence surfaces is broken.
scoring_guide.3: Most scenarios pass but Q-table or handoff round-trip has a subtle drift.
scoring_guide.4: All but one scenario pass. Edge case around empty or boundary state.
scoring_guide.5: All scenarios pass. Persistence is bit-identical across the save/load boundary.
